name: Pumpkin Swirl Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: 2 packages (8 oz each) cream cheese, softened
2 teaspoons grated gingerroot (or 1/2 teaspoon ground ginger)
recipeInstructions:
1. For the crust: Combine crushed gingersnaps, melted butter, and 1 tablespoon of flour in a medium mixing bowl. Press into the bottom and up the sides of an 8-inch or 9-inch springform pan. Set aside.
2. For the filling: Beat cream cheese until smooth in a large mixing bowl. Add sugar, 3 tablespoons of flour, grated gingerroot (or ground ginger), and vanilla; beat on high speed until smooth. Add eggs one at a time, beating on low speed just until combined. Stir in milk.
3. Measure 2 cups of filling into the prepared springform pan. Carefully spoon the pumpkin mixture over the cheese mixture. Use a spatula to gently swirl the batters together, taking care not to disturb the crust.
4. Place the springform pan on a shallow baking pan on the oven rack. Bake in a 350°F oven for 50 minutes (45 to 50 minutes for an 8-inch pan) or until the center is nearly set when shaken.
5. Remove the springform pan from the baking pan and cool on a wire rack for 15 minutes. Use a small metal spatula to loosen the crust from the sides of the pan. Cool for another 30 minutes.
6. Remove the sides of the springform pan and let the cheesecake cool completely, covered, for at least 4 hours. Serves 12 to 16.
